name: Picard Medical, Inc.
business: Picard Medical, Inc. manufactures and sells the SynCardia Total Artificial Heart (TAH), the only FDA and Health Canada approved total artificial heart, which fully replaces the function of a failing human heart. The device is used primarily as a bridge to heart transplantation for patients with biventricular heart failure. The company also develops next-generation technologies such as the Emperor TAH and Unicorn driver.
sellsTo: Hospitals and transplant centers in the United States and Canada

exposures.sentence: Picard Medical, Inc. ("PMI") was incorporated in the state of Delaware on April 8, 2021. ... SynCardia is a medical technology company that manufactures and sells the only U.S. Food and Drug Administration ("FDA") and Health Canada approved Total Artificial Heart ("TAH"), which fully replaces the function of a failing human heart.
The Company is developing the Emperor TAH, a next generation fully implantable artificial heart designed to eliminate the need for external pneumatic drivers.
In 2023, PMI began development of a next generation driver system codenamed Unicorn .

catalysts.event: FDA decision on expanded indications (remove imminent death language, add bridge to candidacy)
catalysts.sentence: The Company currently expects a decision from the FDA regarding this submission in the third quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of Freedom+ driver upgrade
catalysts.sentence: The Company anticipates FDA approval by the end of 2026.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of next generation Freedom Driver
catalysts.sentence: The Company is also working on next generation Freedom and C2 Drivers for which PMI expects to gain FDA approvals during the second quarter in each of 2026 and 2028, respectively.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of next generation C2 Driver (Companion 3)
catalysts.sentence: The Company is also working on next generation Freedom and C2 Drivers for which PMI expects to gain FDA approvals during the second quarter in each of 2026 and 2028, respectively.

catalysts.event: Completion of regulatory testing for Unicorn driver
catalysts.sentence: PMI expects to complete the regulatory testing in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: FDA submission and approval for Unicorn driver
catalysts.sentence: Submission to FDA will be a 180-day PMA supplement, which gives an approval date of approximately the middle of 2027.

catalysts.event: NMPA initial feedback on SynCardia TAH registration in China
catalysts.sentence: SMB expects to receive initial feedback on the status of the application during 2026

catalysts.event: Completion of corrective action for cannula tear issue
catalysts.sentence: Corrective action to address this issue is currently undergoing process validation and is expected to be completed by the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Potential FDA approval for Emperor TAH
catalysts.sentence: Subject to the results of nonclinical testing and regulatory review, PMI may seek FDA approval for Emperor as early as 2028.

tamClaims.claim: 6.8 million people suffer from heart failure in the United States; globally there are 56.2 million people who suffer from the condition.
tamClaims.sentence: According to several studies published in 2024, 6.8 million people suffer from heart failure in the United States; globally there are 56.2 million people who suffer from the condition.

tamClaims.claim: At least 300,000 patients in the United States are living with advanced heart failure.
tamClaims.sentence: Although estimates of the prevalence of advanced heart failure vary anywhere from 5% to 25%, at least 300,000 patients in the United States are living with this condition.

tamClaims.claim: Over 7,500 patients waiting on the heart transplant list and over 4,000 patients are added to the list each year; 4,539 heart transplants performed last year.
tamClaims.sentence: In the United States, the country with the highest number of HTx globally, there are over 7,500 patients waiting on the heart transplant list and over 4,000 patients are added to the list each year. Last year, 4,539 HTx were performed in the United States; worldwide, the number of HTx was estimated at just under 8,200 in 2020.

tamClaims.claim: PMI estimates approximately 15 million people with heart failure per year in the European Union, up to approximately 4.6 million in India, approximately 12 million in China, and approximately 3.75 million in the Middle East.
tamClaims.sentence: PMI estimates approximately 15 million people with heart failure per year in the European Union, up to approximately 4.6 million in India, approximately 12 million in China, and approximately 3.75 million in the Middle East.
